svg.text: Hi, I'm Nilanjana — a Computer Science and Engineering student at the University of Chittagong,
While earning my engineering degree, I found it fascinating how code turns into something that
feels alive — that's what pulled me into development. I build full-stack web applications with
React, Next.js, and MongoDB, and I care just as much about what's underneath: clean data models,
solid auth, systems built to scale past a demo. My goal down the line is simple — to grow into a
software engineer, not just a developer.
Right now I'm juggling two very different builds: J.A.R.V.I.S., a voice assistant powered by
Groq's Llama 3.3 70B and ElevenLabs' text-to-speech, that listens, responds, and even reaches
into my desktop to get things done — and RescueLink, a platform connecting volunteers to
disaster relief missions when it counts, built on Next.js, TypeScript, and MongoDB.
Behind me is UrbanNest, a full-stack property rental marketplace with three user roles, real
Stripe payments, Google OAuth, and dashboards that actually mean something for owners and admins.
If there's a thread running through it all, it's this: I want code that disappears into the
experience — where the engineering discipline behind it never becomes the user's problem.
When I'm not coding, I'm probably debugging my own assumptions about a problem — one commit at
